name: Restore grouped_rows=8 and try a single-argument launch-bounds clue on the accepted hot-band kernel

summary.name: Restore grouped_rows=8 and try a single-argument launch-bounds clue on the accepted hot-band kernel
summary.hypothesis: The grouped-order search now has enough shape to lock in `grouped_rows=8` as the current best L2 setting: both 16 and 4 were worse. That lets us keep the accepted L2 base and revisit the Register-Reuse / compiler-guidance axis in a much safer form than round 19. A single-argument `__launch_bounds__(128)` tells ptxas the real launch width of the hot-band kernel without demanding extra resident blocks, so it is the narrowest reusable compiler clue worth testing next.
summary.expected_bottleneck: Compiler allocation / scheduling quality on top of the accepted grouped-order 128x128 K16 kernel.
summary.risk: The hint may do nothing at all, or it may still perturb codegen negatively. The risk is lower than round 19 because it does not encode a minimum resident-block target.
summary.stop_condition: Stop if runtime regresses back toward the pre-grouped baseline or if the single-argument hint leaves codegen effectively unchanged.

notes: Build passed. Node A must measure this implementation next.
